LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT MORTENSON MATZELLE & MELDRUM, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. A BROKER CAN OWE FIDUCIARY DUTIES TO AN INSURED UNDER SOME CIRCUMSTANCES. WHETHER THOSE CIRCUMSTANCES EXIST IN THIS CASE, AND WHETHER THEY WOULD JUSTIFY SUBMITTING A SEPARATE CLAIM TO THE JURY, ARE ISSUES FOR ANOTHER DAY. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR# 8189 301/PJB

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF WESTERN ENVIRONMENTAL CONSULTANTS, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS SUFFICIENTLY RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO ITS HAVING ADEQUATELY NOTIFIED THE PLAINTIFF CONCERNING THE INSURANCE IT WAS PROVIDING AND WAS ABLE TO PROVIDE. THE OBJECTION WITH RESPECT TO THE SENTENCE IN PARAGRAPH 7 OF MR. KEKULA'S DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. MR. MEREDITH TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Nov-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ESTA L. BRAND OF THE COVINGTON & BURLING LLP, TEL. NO. 591-7022 APPEARING
FOR THE PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY;
ATTY. SAMUEL E. MEREDITH OF VOGL & MEREDITH, LLP, TEL. NO. 398-0200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT M3.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF WESTERN ENVIRONMENTAL CONSULTANTS, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS SUFFICIENTLY RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO ITS HAVING ADEQUATELY NOTIFIED THE PLAINTIFF CONCERNING THE INSURANCE IT WAS PROVIDING AND WAS ABLE TO PROVIDE. THE OBJECTION WITH RESPECT TO THE SENTENCE IN PARAGRAPH 7 OF MR. KEKULA'S DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. MR. MEREDITH TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(g). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT MORTENSON MATZELLE & MELDRUM, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO STRIKE CERTAIN PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR# 8189 =301/PJB/BD

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT MORTENSON MATZELLE & MELDRUM, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. A BROKER CAN OWE FIDUCIARY DUTIES TO AN INSURED UNDER SOME CIRCUMSTANCES. WHETHER THOSE CIRCUMSTANCES EXIST IN THIS CASE, AND WHETHER THEY WOULD JUSTIFY SUBMITTING A SEPARATE CLAIM TO THE JURY, ARE ISSUES FOR ANOTHER DAY. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR# 8189 301/PJB/BD
